supporting_evidence: Collapse-tail detail probes (samples 17-25) show `rawAnimatableOpacity` decaying from 0.232 to 0.009 while `renderedOpacity` stays 0.000, `detailFrameHeightParam` stays 0.000, and `detailVisibleHeight` stays 0.000.
In the same collapse-tail window, `visibilityGateOpen=false` for every sample, so rendered presence remains owned by a closed gate path while an animatable scalar is still changing.
contradicting_evidence: This incident does not include expand transition samples or collapse-start samples (`probe.expand.start/end` and `probe.collapse.start` absent), so intermediate height/opacity values earlier in the transition cannot be confirmed or ruled out from this artifact alone.
missing_evidence: One full expand trace and one full collapse trace, each including transition start through end, with contiguous `detailOpacityProbe`/height samples to verify whether `detailFrameHeightParam` and `detailVisibleHeight` traverse intermediate values under one scalar owner.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Capture a full expand+collapse instrumentation run (start-to-end) for one row and verify that both `detailFrameHeightParam` and `detailVisibleHeight` move through intermediate values in both directions while `renderedOpacity` changes on the same scalar-owned path.

phase_ownership.best_supported_hypothesis: animated-scalar ownership remains unresolved in observed collapse-tail samples
phase_ownership.first_diverging_owner: LogRowCardView rendered detail visibility/opacity gate path
